subject: Cloud Computing
title: How AWS Became the Leader
description: Learn How AWS Became the Leader in one minute.

hook.text: AWS launched in 2006 with two services. Today it offers over 200 and earns $90 billion a year.

buildup.text: Amazon moved first, captured developers, and built a flywheel: more users meant lower prices.

discovery.text: AWS commands about 31% of the cloud market — more than Azure and Google Cloud combined.

twist.text: AWS isn't Amazon's biggest revenue source. But it is by far its biggest profit source.

climax.text: AWS subsidizes Amazon's razor-thin retail margins. Without cloud profits, Amazon looks very different.

punchline.text: A bookstore's side project became tech's most profitable division.

quiz.question: What role does AWS play in Amazon's overall business?
options: It is Amazon's biggest profit source despite not being the biggest revenue source
AWS is a small experimental division within Amazon
AWS generates revenue but has never been profitable
